en.extract:
The grey-cheeked tit-babbler (Mixornis flavicollis) is a species of bird in the family Timaliidae. It is endemic to Java.
Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest.


References

Collar, N. J. & Robson, C. 2007. Family Timaliidae (Babblers)  pp. 70 – 291 in; del Hoyo, J., Elliott, A. & Christie, D.A. eds. Handbook of the Birds of the World, Vol. 12. Picathartes to Tits and Chickadees. Lynx Edicions, Barcelona.

The '''grey-cheeked tit-babbler''' ('''''Mixornis flavicollis''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Timaliidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Java]].<ref name="iucn" /><ref name="Moyle2012">{{cite journal|last1= Moyle|first1= R.G.|last2= Andersen|first2= M.J.|last3= Oliveros|first3= C.H.|last4= Steinheimer|first4= F.D.|last5= Reddy|first5= S.|title= Phylogeny and Biogeography of the Core Babblers (Aves: Timaliidae)|journal= Systematic Biology|volume= 61|issue= 4|year= 2012|pages= 631–651|doi= 10.1093/sysbio/sys027|pmid= 22328569|doi-access= free}}</ref>

Its natural [[habitat]] is [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]].
